{{Quote|I've had a ''good'' life, I think.|Aron Harcourt|<ref name="e28" />}}
'''Aron Harcourt''' was a disgraced former [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], who served [[Administrator/Legends|administratively]] on a [[Harcourt's outpost|remote outpost]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. Harcourt was once renowned as a hero of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], commanding the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Anya Karu]] - ''but the'' Anya Karu'' was destroyed by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]] by [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and he was forced to abandon ship, leaving it derelict on a desolate planet. He was blamed for the loss of the ''Anya Karu'' in the subsequent [[Inquiry on the sabotaging of the Anya Karu|inquiry]] and removed from the command structure of the Imperial [[Navy/Legends|Navy]]. By 0 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], Harcourt decided to have a [[holograph]]ic recording of his late wife, [[Janelle Harcourt|Janelle]], recovered from the ''Anya Karu,'' before the wreck was scuttled. Lacking the requisite finances, Harcourt nonetheless enlisted the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to recover the [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]]. Although the recording was retrieved and Harcourt was able to view it once more, his inability to pay the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter resulted in Fett [[Death/Legends|terminating]] his life.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Normally that sort of thing wouldn't be allowed. But Captain ''Aron Harcourt'' was a hero of the Empire once. My ship and I rated certain ''privileges''.|Aron Harcourt, discussing his use of hunter droids as bodyguards|<ref name="e28" />}}
Aron Harcourt was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who began a [[Navy/Legends|naval]] career during the [[Great Peace of the Republic]]. While studying at an academy, he met a woman named [[Janelle Harcourt|Janelle]], whom he later [[Marriage/Legends|married]]. Harcourt ultimately served in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and was given the [[Captain/Legends|captaincy]] of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Anya Karu]]''. He gained a reputation as a hero of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and was bequeathed a [[RHTC-560 Hunter Trainer]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and [[RHTC-560 HT Drone]]s by a [[Unidentified Rodian warlord|Rodian warlord]]. With nothing for the droids to hunt, Harcourt put them to use as his personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], which normally would have been against naval regulation but was allowed due to his renown.<ref name="e28" />

By [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Note">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Boba Fett]]'' dates [[Empire 28|''Empire'' 28]] to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. {{CSWECite|II|18|Harcourt, Captain Aron}} establishes Janelle died after the ''Anya Karu'' was wrecked, and Harcourt mentions in the comic that Janelle was dead more than three years during his interaction with Boba Fett, which would have to place both events as occurring by 3 BBY at the latest.</ref> [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]] attacked the ''Anya Karu''. As a result, Harcourt was forced to give the order to abandon [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. The ''Anya Kuro'' crashed on a [[Unidentified planet (Anya Karu)|desolate planet]] as a result of the sabotage, leaving Harcourt subject to an [[Inquiry on the sabotaging of the Anya Karu|inquiry]]. Despite his [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of service, Harcourt was blamed for the loss of the ship—the captain speculated, however, that the decision was a [[Politics/Legends|political]] attempt to cover up the fact that the Alliance had been able to sabotage an Imperial [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]].<ref name="e28" /> At some later point following the ''Anya Karu''{{'s}} wreckage and by 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Note" /> Janelle [[Death/Legends|died]], leaving only a brief [[holograph]]ic recording of herself to be remembered by. Harcourt treasured the recording and kept it in his personal quarters on the ''Anya Karu''.<ref name="e28" />

[[File:HarcourtDeath.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Harcourt, shot dead by Boba Fett]]
Ultimately Harcourt was removed from the Imperial Navy command structure and placed in charge of a [[Harcourt's outpost|remote outpost]].<ref name="cswe" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="BFOmnibus" /> he decided to have his hologram of Janelle recovered. The [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Liberator (Imperial I-class)|''Adjudicator'']] had been dispatched to the barren [[Planet/Legends|world]] to scuttle what was left of the wreckage of the ''Anya Karu'', if it was destroyed, he would never see his wife again. Harcourt's only hope was to enlist the services of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], despite only having enough [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] to pay for half of Fett's fee. Using deceit, the former captain made a deal to pay half of the fee before retrieval of the hologram and another half later. In truth, however, he paid Fett all the money he had up front. The [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter made his way to the wreck of the ''Anya Karu'', braving [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] patrols and Harcourt's "bodyguards" before successfully finding the recording of Janelle and bringing it back to the outpost.<ref name="e28" />

When Fett presented him with the holorecording, Harcourt immediately began watching the short loop of his wife and reminisced about their first meeting. The former captain was pleased with the bounty hunter's work, but when Fett demanded payment, Harcourt was forced to admit that he had nothing with which to pay the bounty hunter. He suggested that he would not tell anyone else if Fett overlooked the shortfall, so as to preserve the bounty hunter's reputation, but Harcourt soon realized that Fett was not interested in such a bargain and intended to kill him for the transgression. Reflecting on the holorecording of his wife once more, Harcourt conceded that he had lived a good life, albeit a lonely one in the wake of Janelle's death and the loss of his command. He admitted to Fett that he had only wanted to see his wife's visage and hear her say his name once more. As Harcourt professed his love for his wife one more time, Fett shot him in the back of the head, ending the former captain's life as the holorecording repeated its short loop.<ref name="e28" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I loved her so very much.|Aron Harcourt professes his love for his wife, Janelle|<ref name="e28" />}}
Harcourt was an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of great distinction, hailed in his own time as a "hero of the Empire." His position afforded him privileges above and beyond those of a normal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] captain, although he could not save himself from being held accountable as a scapegoat for his ship succumbing to Alliance sabotage. Harcourt was critical of the Empire in that instance, musing on the fact that his three [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of dedicated service were not even a consideration during the ''Anya Karu'' inquiry. Nevertheless, the captain took great pride in his ship even after it had been lost, keeping a model of it on his desk after being reassigned.

Harcourt loved his wife dearly, and the feeling was reciprocated by Janelle. After her death, he valued his brief recording of her, to such a degree that he was willing to have his life ended just to see and hear the recording again.<ref name="e28" />

He had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], which was graying at the temples, blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and light skin.<ref name="e28" />

==Equipment==
Harcourt wore the standard olive-green [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|uniform]] of an Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officer]] while administrating his remote outpost.<ref name="e28" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aron Harcourt first appeared in [[Empire 28|''Empire'' 28]] ([[2004]]), written by [[Ron Marz]] and penciled by [[Adriana Melo]]. The character was later referenced in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[2008]]).
